# UC-19.2.34 · Proxmox LVM-Thin Data and Metadata Utilization Guardrails

## Description

LVM-thin provisioning on Proxmox allows oversubscription of guest disks. High data or metadata utilization on the thin pool risks immediate write failures for all guests on that pool even though individual VM graphs still look healthy.

## Value

Prevents thin-pool exhaustion incidents by monitoring pool-level metadata and data ratios, not only per-VM allocation.

## Implementation

Poll storage status per node and index `data` and `meta` utilization percentages returned by the API (field names vary slightly by version—normalize in the input). Alert at conservative thresholds (for example 80% data, 70% meta). Include runbook steps for `lvextend` and guest disk review.

## SPL

```spl
index=virtualization sourcetype="proxmox:storage" type="lvmthin" earliest=-1h
| where data_pct>=80 OR meta_pct>=70
| stats latest(data_pct) as data_pct, latest(meta_pct) as meta_pct by storage, node
| sort -data_pct
```

## Visualization

Heatmap (node × storage), Table (pools breaching threshold), Single value (worst pool).

## References

- [Proxmox VE Storage — LVM thin](https://pve.proxmox.com/wiki/Storage:_LVM_Thin)
